ocr.text: The analysis of participant characteristics reveals critical insights into the demographic profile of the study population. Notably,
the mean age of participants is 55.1 years, with a standard deviation of 10.7, indicating a diverse age range that encompasses
both younger and older adults. This age distribution is significant as it allows for a more comprehensive understanding of the
factors influencing the study's primary outcomes.
Characteristics  Mean (SD)
Age, years  55.1 (10.7)
History of prior malignancy  2 (2.7)
Family history of malignancy  3 (4.1)
Diameter, mm  10.4 (3.5)
Distance from lesion to the pleural surface, mm  9.4 (4.9)
Preoperative localization time, minutes  22.2 (5.0)
Intraoperative localization time, minutes  17.4 (2.3)
Stain – lesion distance, mm  5.1 (3.1)
In examining the characteristics of the participants, it is essential to consider how age may intersect with other variables, such
as health status and socioeconomic factors, which are crucial in interpreting the data effectively. The mean (SD) metric provides
a foundational framework for assessing the variability within the age group, highlighting the potential for differing experiences
and outcomes based on age-related factors. Furthermore, understanding these characteristics is vital for tailoring interventions
and recommendations that address the unique needs of this population. As the study progresses, continued analysis of these
characteristics will enhance the robustness of the findings and facilitate a nuanced discussion around age-related implications in
the context of the research objectives.
